is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study of uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria in Malawian children; DHFR and DHPS genotyping of P. falciparum isolates.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Analyzed DHFR codons 51, 59, 108 and DHPS codons 437, 540, 581, 613 — all curated target codons (Pfdhfr 51,59,108; Pfdhps 437,540,581,613).

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Baseline prevalence reported: DHFR Ile-51/Glu-540 in 70%, Arg-59/Glu-540 65%, DHFR triple mutant 78%, quintuple 64% among 100 randomly selected samples; also counts in Tables 2-4.

sub_country_location.evidence: All samples from a single site: Ndirande Health Centre in Blantyre, Malawi. This is a single sub-national site so resolution is fine, but frequencies tie to one location only — actually sub-country resolution IS met (Blantyre/Ndirande).

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: Children enrolled and treated over the course of 1 year in a prospective trial; collection window within a single year (<=3y). Study conducted around 1999 (presented at ASTMH Dec 1999).

african_field_samples.evidence: Human clinical field isolates collected from children presenting to Ndirande Health Centre, Blantyre, Malawi (African country).

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary empirical study: prospective double-blind placebo-controlled trial with molecular genotyping of collected samples.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Samples are pretreatment samples from a clinical trial whose clinical results are reported elsewhere (Sulo et al., unpublished data); the same collection may be published in related papers.

supplement.evidence: Frequency data and codon results appear in main text and Tables 2-5; no critical extraction data pointed to supplementary material.

assessment.summary: Prospective double-blind trial in children at Ndirande Health Centre, Blantyre, Malawi, genotyping P. falciparum DHFR (51,59,108) and DHPS (437,540,581,613) mutations in relation to SP and chlorproguanil-dapsone treatment outcomes. Baseline prevalence frequencies reported for a single site over a ~1-year window. Sub-country resolution is met (single named site), temporal resolution within 3y, African field samples, primary study, target markers with extractable frequencies. Eligibility hinges on whether frequencies are reported per-locus; genotype-level frequencies are given but individual codon frequencies (e.g., Arg-59, Glu-540) are extractable. Marked ineligible only due to uncertainty in per-locus extractability being reported mainly as combined genotypes.
exclusion_reasons: Frequencies are largely reported as combined genotype categories (triple/quintuple mutant) rather than clean per-locus numerator/denominator counts, though some single-codon prevalences (e.g., Arg-59 65%, Glu-540) are extractable; per-locus extraction is partly ambiguous.
